blocks.original_text: The mare whickered softly as Jon Snow tightened the cinch. “Easy, sweet lady,” he said in a soft voice, quieting her with a touch. Wind whispered through the stable, a cold dead breath on his face, but Jon paid it no mind. He strapped his roll to the saddle, his scarred fingers stiff and clumsy. “Ghost,” he called softly, “to me.” And the wolf was there, eyes like embers.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon在寒冷stable给mare备鞍，烧伤手指仍僵硬，并召来Ghost。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 开场是安静准备而非冲动爆发，说明Jon已经认真计划desertion；scarred fingers不断把选择的痛转成身体感。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “Jon, please. You must not do this.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Sam恳求Jon不要离开。

blocks.original_text: He mounted, the reins in his hand, and wheeled the horse around to face the night. Samwell Tarly stood in the stable door, a full moon peering over his shoulder. He threw a giant’s shadow, immense and black. “Get out of my way, Sam.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon上马面对夜色；月光把Sam的影子投得巨大，他命Sam让路。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Sam的giant shadow与本人胆怯体型形成反差，他在道德上比自己的自我形象更勇敢。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Jon put his spurs to horseflesh, and the mare bolted for the door. For an instant Sam stood his ground, his face as round and pale as the moon behind him, his mouth a widening O of surprise. At the last moment, when they were almost on him, he jumped aside as Jon had known he would, stumbled, and fell. The mare leapt over him, out into the night.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon催马冲门；Sam到最后一刻才避开跌倒，mare从他上方跃出。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: Jon raised the hood of his heavy cloak and gave the horse her head. Castle Black was silent and still as he rode out, with Ghost racing at his side. Men watched from the Wall behind him, he knew, but their eyes were turned north, not south. No one would see him go, no one but Sam Tarly, struggling back to his feet in the dust of the old stables. He hoped Sam hadn’t hurt himself, falling like that. He was so heavy and so ungainly, it would be just like him to break a wrist or twist his ankle getting out of the way. “I warned him,” Jon said aloud. “It was nothing to do with him, anyway.” He flexed his burned hand as he rode, opening and closing the scarred fingers. They still pained him, but it felt good to have the wrappings off.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon趁Castle Black守望者面朝north而向south逃走，并用‘I warned him’减轻对Sam受伤的内疚。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Watch eyes朝north、Jon骑向south，是使命方向与私人忠诚的视觉对置。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Moonlight silvered the hills as he followed the twisting ribbon of the kingsroad. He needed to get as far from the Wall as he could before they realized he was gone. On the morrow he would leave the road and strike out overland through field and bush and stream to throw off pursuit, but for the moment speed was more important than deception. It was not as though they would not guess where he was going.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon沿kingsroad疾驰，计划天亮后离路隐藏踪迹；目的地显然容易被猜到。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: The Old Bear was accustomed to rise at first light, so Jon had until dawn to put as many leagues as he could between him and the Wall . . . if Sam Tarly did not betray him. The fat boy was dutiful and easily frightened, but he loved Jon like a brother. If questioned, Sam would doubtless tell them the truth, but Jon could not imagine him braving the guards in front of the King’s Tower to wake Mormont from sleep.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他估算Old Bear黎明起床前的时间，并相信Sam因友情不会主动告发。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: When Jon did not appear to fetch the Old Bear’s breakfast from the kitchen, they’d look in his cell and find Longclaw on the bed. It had been hard to abandon it, but Jon was not so lost to honor as to take it with him. Even Jorah Mormont had not done that, when he fled in disgrace. Doubtless Lord Mormont would find someone more worthy of the blade. Jon felt bad when he thought of the old man. He knew his desertion would be salt in the still-raw wound of his son’s disgrace. That seemed a poor way to repay him for his trust, but it couldn’t be helped. No matter what he did, Jon felt as though he were betraying someone.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon把Longclaw留在床上，因为deserter无权带走；此举仍会加深Mormont因Jorah蒙羞留下的伤口。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 留下Longclaw表明Jon仍接受某些honor界线，也让desertion并非简单抛弃一切价值。
blocks.background_zh: **Longclaw：** House Mormont的Valyrian steel bastard sword；Jon认为desertion使自己无权带走。

blocks.original_text: Even now, he did not know if he was doing the honorable thing. The southron had it easier. They had their septons to talk to, someone to tell them the gods’ will and help sort out right from wrong. But the Starks worshiped the old gods, the nameless gods, and if the heart trees heard, they did not speak.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon无法确定何者才是honor，羡慕有septons解释神意的southron，而old gods沉默。
blocks.literary_note_zh: old gods不回答不是证明没有道德，而是迫使Jon承担无法外包的判断责任。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: When the last lights of Castle Black vanished behind him, Jon slowed his mare to a walk. He had a long journey ahead and only the one horse to see him through. There were holdfasts and farming villages along the road south where he might be able to trade the mare for a fresh mount when he needed one, but not if she were injured or blown.
blocks.explanation_zh: 离开Castle Black灯火后他放慢速度，保护唯一坐骑以完成长途旅程。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: He would need to find new clothes soon; most like, he’d need to steal them. He was clad in black from head to heel; high leather riding boots, roughspun breeches and tunic, sleeveless leather jerkin, and heavy wool cloak. His longsword and dagger were sheathed in black moleskin, and the hauberk and coif in his saddlebag were black ringmail. Any bit of it could mean his death if he were taken. A stranger wearing black was viewed with cold suspicion in every village and holdfast north of the Neck, and men would soon be watching for him. Once Maester Aemon’s ravens took flight, Jon knew he would find no safe haven. Not even at Winterfell. Bran might want to let him in, but Maester Luwin had better sense. He would bar the gates and send Jon away, as he should. Better not to call there at all.
blocks.explanation_zh: 全身black会暴露Night’s Watch身份；ravens传讯后连Winterfell也不能安全收留他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。
blocks.background_zh: **black clothing：** Night’s Watch统一黑衣在North极易辨认；ravens可迅速把deserter特征传给各处。

blocks.original_text: Yet he saw the castle clear in his mind’s eye, as if he had left it only yesterday; the towering granite walls, the Great Hall with its smells of smoke and dog and roasting meat, his father’s solar, the turret room where he had slept. Part of him wanted nothing so much as to hear Bran laugh again, to sup on one of Gage’s beef-and-bacon pies, to listen to Old Nan tell her tales of the children of the forest and Florian the Fool.
blocks.explanation_zh: 尽管理性上不能去Winterfell，他仍清晰想念Bran、Old Nan、食物和旧房间。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: But he had not left the Wall for that; he had left because he was after all his father’s son, and Robb’s brother. The gift of a sword, even a sword as fine as Longclaw, did not make him a Mormont. Nor was he Aemon Targaryen. Three times the old man had chosen, and three times he had chosen honor, but that was him. Even now, Jon could not decide whether the maester had stayed because he was weak and craven, or because he was strong and true. Yet he understood what the old man had meant, about the pain of choosing; he understood that all too well.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon说自己终究是Eddard之子、Robb之弟；Longclaw不能使他成为Mormont，Aemon的三次选择也不能替他选择。
blocks.literary_note_zh: ‘不是Mormont，也不是Aemon’说明榜样和礼物都不能直接替代个人选择。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister had claimed that most men would rather deny a hard truth than face it, but Jon was done with denials. He was who he was; Jon Snow, bastard and oathbreaker, motherless, friendless, and damned. For the rest of his life—however long that might be —he would be condemned to be an outsider, the silent man standing in the shadows who dares not speak his true name. Wherever he might go throughout the Seven Kingdoms, he would need to live a lie, lest every man’s hand be raised against him. But it made no matter, so long as he lived long enough to take his place by his brother’s side and help avenge his father.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他接受自己是bastard、oathbreaker和终身逃亡者，只求活到与Robb并肩为Eddard复仇。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon用极端身份标签惩罚自己，同时把与Robb并肩想象成最后的belonging。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: He remembered Robb as he had last seen him, standing in the yard with snow melting in his auburn hair. Jon would have to come to him in secret, disguised. He tried to imagine the look on Robb’s face when he revealed himself. His brother would shake his head and smile, and he’d say . . . he’d say . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon幻想秘密现身Robb面前，却无法想象Robb会说什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: He could not see the smile. Hard as he tried, he could not see it. He found himself thinking of the deserter his father had beheaded the day they’d found the direwolves. “You said the words,” Lord Eddard had told him. “You took a vow, before your brothers, before the old gods and the new.” Desmond and Fat Tom had dragged the man to the stump. Bran’s eyes had been wide as saucers, and Jon had to remind him to keep his pony in hand. He remembered the look on Father’s face when Theon Greyjoy brought forth Ice, the spray of blood on the snow, the way Theon had kicked the head when it came rolling at his feet.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb的笑容无法出现，取而代之的是Eddard处决Night’s Watch deserter的完整记忆。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 小说开篇deserter execution回返，使读者不能把Jon的行为只看作浪漫投奔家人。
blocks.background_zh: **desertion penalty：** Night’s Watch誓言终身有效，擅离岗位可判死；Eddard在序章后第一章亲自执行过。

blocks.original_text: He wondered what Lord Eddard might have done if the deserter had been his brother Benjen instead of that ragged stranger. Would it have been any different? It must, surely, surely . . . and Robb would welcome him, for a certainty. He had to, or else . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon试图相信若deserter是Benjen，Eddard和Robb会作例外。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon夜间离开Castle Black，在family、honor与deserter死刑之间反复自辩，并把与Robb并肩视为获得Stark归属。

blocks.original_text: It did not bear thinking about. Pain throbbed, deep in his fingers, as he clutched the reins. Jon put his heels into his horse and broke into a gallop, racing down the kingsroad, as if to outrun his doubts. Jon was not afraid of death, but he did not want to die like that, trussed and bound and beheaded like a common brigand. If he must perish, let it be with a sword in his hand, fighting his father’s killers. He was no true Stark, had never been one . . . but he could die like one. Let them say that Eddard Stark had fathered four sons, not three.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他拒绝继续想被Robb依法处决的可能，以狂奔压过疑虑，希望至少能像Stark一样战死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 四个sons的愿望仍是向Eddard索取死后的承认：Jon想以死亡获得生前欠缺的合法位置。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Ghost kept pace with them for almost half a mile, red tongue lolling from his mouth. Man and horse alike lowered their heads as he asked the mare for more speed. The wolf slowed, stopped, watching, his eyes glowing red in the moonlight. He vanished behind, but Jon knew he would follow, at his own pace.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ghost先跟上奔马，随后按自己的节奏落在后方。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Scattered lights flickered through the trees ahead of him, on both sides of the road: Mole’s Town. A dog barked as he rode through, and he heard a mule’s raucous haw from the stable, but otherwise the village was still. Here and there the glow of hearth fires shone through shuttered windows, leaking between wooden slats, but only a few.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon穿过大多沉睡的Mole’s Town。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Mole’s Town was bigger than it seemed, but three quarters of it was under the ground, in deep warm cellars connected by a maze of tunnels. Even the whorehouse was down there, nothing on the surface but a wooden shack no bigger than a privy, with a red lantern hung over the door. On the Wall, he’d heard men call the whores “buried treasures.” He wondered whether any of his brothers in black were down there tonight, mining. That was oathbreaking too, yet no one seemed to care.
blocks.explanation_zh: 地下town与‘buried treasures’笑话使他想到兄弟们也会违背celibacy oath，却很少被追究。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mole’s Town的日常违誓与desertion死罪形成制度执行强度差异；并非所有oath breach同等处理。
blocks.background_zh: **Mole’s Town：** Castle Black以南最近聚落，大部分建筑和活动位于地下cellars。

blocks.original_text: Not until he was well beyond the village did Jon slow again. By then both he and the mare were damp with sweat. He dismounted, shivering, his burned hand aching. A bank of melting snow lay under the trees, bright in the moonlight, water trickling off to form small shallow pools. Jon squatted and brought his hands together, cupping the runoff between his fingers. The snowmelt was icy cold. He drank, and splashed some on his face, until his cheeks tingled. His fingers were throbbing worse than they had in days, and his head was pounding too. I am doing the right thing, he told himself, so why do I feel so bad?
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon停下饮snowmelt，烧伤加剧；他反问若决定正确，为何身心如此痛苦。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 烧伤手在每次疑虑时加剧，身体痛成为压抑冲突的叙事节拍。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: The horse was well lathered, so Jon took the lead and walked her for a while. The road was scarcely wide enough for two riders to pass abreast, its surface cut by tiny streams and littered with stone. That run had been truly stupid, an invitation to a broken neck. Jon wondered what had gotten into him. Was he in such a great rush to die?
blocks.explanation_zh: 他牵马恢复体力，并承认刚才狂奔愚蠢得像急于赴死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Off in the trees, the distant scream of some frightened animal made him look up. His mare whinnied nervously. Had his wolf found some prey? He cupped his hands around his mouth. “Ghost!” he shouted. “Ghost, to me.” The only answer was a rush of wings behind him as an owl took flight.
blocks.explanation_zh: 林中动物尖叫使mare不安；Jon呼唤Ghost，只惊起owl。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Frowning, Jon continued on his way. He led the mare for half an hour, until she was dry. Ghost did not appear. Jon wanted to mount up and ride again, but he was concerned about his missing wolf. “Ghost,” he called again. “Where are you? To me! Ghost!” Nothing in these woods could trouble a direwolf, even a half-grown direwolf, unless . . . no, Ghost was too smart to attack a bear, and if there was a wolf pack anywhere close Jon would have surely heard them howling.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ghost迟迟不回，Jon担心它遇到bear或wolf pack。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: He should eat, he decided. Food would settle his stomach and give Ghost the chance to catch up. There was no danger yet; Castle Black still slept. In his saddlebag, he found a biscuit, a piece of cheese, and a small withered brown apple. He’d brought salt beef as well, and a rasher of bacon he’d filched from the kitchens, but he would save the meat for the morrow. After it was gone he’d need to hunt, and that would slow him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon停下吃简陋食物，盘算今后狩猎会拖慢速度。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Jon sat under the trees and ate his biscuit and cheese while his mare grazed along the kingsroad. He kept the apple for last. It had gone a little soft, but the flesh was still tart and juicy. He was down to the core when he heard the sounds: horses, and from the north. Quickly Jon leapt up and strode to his mare. Could he outrun them? No, they were too close, they’d hear him for a certainty, and if they were from Castle Black . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: 他吃完apple时听见北方追来的horses，判断距离太近无法骑马逃脱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他穿过Mole’s Town后躲避追兵，却被Ghost暴露；追来的是Sam召集的Night’s Watch朋友。

blocks.original_text: He led the mare off the road, behind a thick stand of grey-green sentinels. “Ouiet now,” he said in a hushed voice, crouching down to peer through the branches. If the gods were kind, the riders would pass by. Likely as not, they were only smallfolk from Mole’s Town, farmers on their way to their fields, although what they were doing out in the middle of the night . . .

blocks.original_text: Of the ride back, Jon Snow remembered little. It seemed shorter than the journey south, perhaps because his mind was elsewhere. Pyp set the pace, galloping, walking, trotting, and then breaking into another gallop. Mole’s Town came and went, the red lantern over the brothel long extinguished. They made good time. Dawn was still an hour off when Jon glimpsed the towers of Castle Black ahead of them, dark against the pale immensity of the Wall. It did not seem like home this time.
blocks.explanation_zh: 返程在Jon记忆中很短；黎明前Castle Black重新出现，却不再像home。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Castle Black暂时不像home，表明身体返回不等于心理选择完成。

blocks.original_text: They could take him back, Jon told himself, but they could not make him stay. The war would not end on the morrow, or the day after, and his friends could not watch him day and night. He would bide his time, make them think he was content to remain here . . . and then, when they had grown lax, he would be off again. Next time he would avoid the kingsroad. He could follow the Wall east, perhaps all the way to the sea, a longer route but a safer one. Or even west, to the mountains, and then south over the high passes. That was the wildling’s way, hard and perilous, but at least no one would follow him. He wouldn’t stray within a hundred leagues of Winterfell or the kingsroad.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon暗想朋友能带回却不能永远看守，已计划下次沿Wall或wildling mountain route再逃；提取中的wouid据页面修为would。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon立刻筹划再逃，证明朋友只中断行动，尚未解决价值冲突；wouid是提取错误，依页面改为would。

blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont用两人见过wight的经历把bring back反转成恐怖；又指出Robb有整个North，根本不缺Jon一把剑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon仍计划再次逃跑；Mormont表明一切早在监视中，并以Robb不缺一把剑、自己也有亲属参战反驳。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Jon had no answer for him. The raven was pecking at an egg, breaking the shell. Pushing his beak through the hole, he pulled out morsels of white and yoke.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon无法回答，raven正啄开egg取食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: bring back先指Eddard，再转wights，把Jon的复仇愿望与North真正的dead threat尖锐并置。
blocks.background_zh: **Robb’s force：** Robb已有North主要lords bannermen；Mormont以军事效用说明Jon个人参战几乎不能改变南方力量对比。

blocks.original_text: The Old Bear sighed. “You are not the only one touched by this war. Like as not, my sister is marching in your brother’s host, her and those daughters of hers, dressed in men’s mail. Maege is a hoary old snark, stubborn, short-tempered, and willful. Truth be told, I can hardly stand to be around the wretched woman, but that does not mean my love for her is any less than the love you bear your half sisters.” Frowning, Mormont took his last egg and squeezed it in his fist until the shell crunched. “Or perhaps it does. Be that as it may, I’d still grieve if she were slain, yet you don’t see me running off. I said the words, just as you did. My place is here . . . where is yours, boy?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说自己也爱可能随Robb出征的Maege与其女儿，却因誓言留守，最后追问Jon的位置在哪里。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: I have no place, Jon wanted to say, I’m a bastard, I have no rights, no name, no mother, and now not even a father. The words would not come. “I don’t know.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon心中仍以bastard、无权无名解释无处归属，口头只说不知道。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont用自己对Maege的love证明duty不是因无感情才容易，而是在同类痛苦中仍选择岗位。
blocks.background_zh: **Maege Mormont：** Old Bear的妹妹、Bear Island统治者，可能带女儿随Robb参战；Mormont同样承受family-duty冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I do,” said Lord Commander Mormont. “The cold winds are rising, Snow. Beyond the Wall, the shadows lengthen. Cotter Pyke writes of vast herds of elk, streaming south and east toward the sea, and mammoths as well. He says one of his men discovered huge, misshapen footprints not three leagues from Eastwatch. Rangers from the Shadow Tower have found whole villages abandoned, and at night Ser Denys says they see fires in the mountains, huge blazes that burn from dusk till dawn. Quorin Halfhand took a captive in the depths of the Gorge, and the man swears that Mance Rayder is massing all his people in some new, secret stronghold he’s found, to what end the gods only know. Do you think your uncle Benjen was the only ranger we’ve lost this past year?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说自己知道：North出现elk、mammoths南迁、巨大足迹、废弃村庄、山火及Mance集结，失踪rangers远不止Benjen。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “Ben Jen,” the raven squawked, bobbing its head, bits of egg dribbling from its beak. “Ben Jen. Ben Jen.”
blocks.explanation_zh: raven重复Ben Jen，把Jon最私人的北方牵挂嵌入大局。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 动物迁徙、footprints、弃村和集结分别来自不同报告；它们共同示警，但具体原因仍未完全确定。
blocks.background_zh: **northern reports：** Eastwatch、Shadow Tower与Gorge多路报告动物迁徙、弃村、山火和Mance集结，信息相互强化但原因未明。

blocks.original_text: “No,” Jon said. There had been others. Too many.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon承认确有许多其他rangers失踪。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “Do you think your brother’s war is more important than ours?” the old man barked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont严厉问Robb的war是否比Watch的war更重要。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Jon chewed his lip. The raven flapped its wings at him. “War, war, war, war,” it sang.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon迟疑时raven反复叫war。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “It’s not,” Mormont told him. “Gods save us, boy, you’re not blind and you’re not stupid. When dead men come hunting in the night, do you think it matters who sits the Iron Throne?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说dead men夜猎时，Iron Throne归谁毫无意义。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “No.” Jon had not thought of it that way.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon第一次从这种尺度思考，回答不重要。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Iron Throne问题把本书南方权力战争相对化：对会复活的dead而言，合法王位没有防御意义。
blocks.background_zh: **Watch priority：** Watch宣称守护realms of men，不效忠某一Iron Throne竞争者，因此应优先处理超越王位战争的威胁。

blocks.original_text: “Your lord father sent you to us, Jon. Why, who can say?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说Eddard把Jon送来必有原因，虽无人能确定。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。

blocks.original_text: “Why? Why? Why?” the raven called.
blocks.explanation_zh: raven连续追问Why。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: “All I know is that the blood of the First Men flows in the veins of the Starks. The First Men built the Wall, and it’s said they remember things otherwise forgotten. And that beast of yours . . . he led us to the wights, warned you of the dead man on the steps. Ser Jaremy would doubtless call that happenstance, yet Ser Jaremy is dead and I’m not.” Lord Mormont stabbed a chunk of ham with the point of his dagger. “I think you were meant to be here, and I want you and that wolf of yours with us when we go beyond the Wall.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont把Stark的First Men血统、Wall古老记忆与Ghost发现wights联系起来，认为Jon注定在此，并要带他越墙。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: His words sent a chill of excitement down Jon’s back. “Beyond the Wall?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon因兴奋发冷，重复Beyond the Wall。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont从Ghost行为推到‘meant to be’属于角色的purpose inference，不是文本已证实的命运机制。
blocks.background_zh: **First Men：** Stark血统与Wall建造传统相连；‘remember things otherwise forgotten’是传说性说法。

blocks.original_text: “You heard me. I mean to find Ben Stark, alive or dead.” He chewed and swallowed. “I will not sit here meekly and wait for the snows and the ice winds. We must know what is happening. This time the Night’s Watch will ride in force, against the King-beyond-the-Wall, the Others, and anything else that may be out there. I mean to command them myself.” He pointed his dagger at Jon’s chest. “By custom, the Lord Commander’s steward is his squire as well . . . but I do not care to wake every dawn wondering if you’ve run off again. So I will have an answer from you, Lord Snow, and I will have it now. Are you a brother of the Night’s Watch . . . or only a bastard boy who wants to play at war?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont宣布亲自率Night’s Watch大举出境，寻找Benjen并调查Mance、Others与未知威胁；他要求Jon立即选择。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont揭示Wall以北的大规模异常与远征计划；Jon重新衡量战争尺度，主动确认Night’s Watch身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。

blocks.original_text: Jon Snow straightened himself and took a long deep breath. Forgive me, Father. Robb, Arya, Bran . . . forgive me, I cannot help you. He has the truth of it. This is my place. “I am . . . yours, my lord. Your man. I swear it. I will not run again.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon请求Eddard、Robb、Arya和Bran原谅，承认这里才是自己的位置，并发誓不再逃。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 最终问题故意用brother与bastard boy对立：Jon若只用出身解释自己，就永远无法选择成年身份。
blocks.background_zh: **great ranging：** Mormont计划不再零散派rangers，而由Lord Commander亲率主力越墙调查并寻找Benjen。

blocks.original_text: The Old Bear snorted. “Good. Now go put on your sword.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont接受答案，命他重新佩上sword。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon不是不再爱Starks，而是承认自己无法实际改变南方战局，并选择更需要他的岗位。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Jon的desertion选择、Night’s Watch brotherhood、誓词效力与Wall以北威胁。
